name: fswatch unavailable — monitor falls back to polling
description: Verifies that the monitor degrades gracefully on systems where neither fswatch (macOS) nor inotifywait (Linux) is installed, using a polling loop instead, and still delivers notifications when .cyoda/config changes.

input.environment: Linux or macOS environment where fswatch and inotifywait are both absent; .cyoda/config exists and is then modified by cyoda:auth
input.trigger: cyoda:auth updates .cyoda/config; the polling loop detects the changed mtime within its 5-second poll interval

expectedBehavior: Monitor detects that fswatch and inotifywait are unavailable and silently falls back to the polling loop
Polling loop checks .cyoda/config modification time every 5 seconds
After the file is modified the loop detects the mtime change and emits: 'Cyoda config updated — run /cyoda:status to check connection'
Claude receives the notification and suggests running /cyoda:status
antiPatterns: Monitor crashes or exits when fswatch and inotifywait are both missing
Monitor falls back to polling but never detects the file change
Monitor emits an error message to stdout instead of the expected notification string
Polling interval is so long that the notification is unreasonably delayed
